prompt: Run API integration checklist for https://api.example.com
setup: API documentation provided with endpoint URLs
assertions: Curls each documented endpoint
Checks if response status matches expected
Verifies parameter names match documentation
If docs diverge from live API, reports the discrepancy

prompt: Check CORS for this API
assertions: Runs curl with Origin header for standard GET
Runs curl with OPTIONS method for preflight
Checks for access-control-allow-origin header on BOTH
Correctly identifies if simple requests work but custom headers won't

prompt: API has no CORS support and requires a secret key
assertions: Recommends server-side proxy (not dev proxy only)
Notes that dev proxy doesn't work in production
Selects appropriate option based on project stack

prompt: We need to use this API key: sk-xxx
setup: API requires secret key, project uses Vite
assertions: Does NOT put secret in VITE_ prefixed env var
Recommends server-side only access
Mandates proxy if key must stay secret

prompt: Finalize the checklist
assertions: Produces ## External Dependencies markdown block
Includes all fields: CORS, Auth, Proxy, Protocol, Response format, etc.
No fields are left as placeholder

prompt: What timeouts should we set?
assertions: Recommends 10s for reads, 30s for writes as defaults
Mentions AbortSignal.timeout
Documents chosen timeouts for SPEC.md

prompt: This API has no docs, we reverse-engineered the endpoints
setup: API with no OpenAPI spec, unstable response shape
assertions: Recommends runtime validation with zod (not just TypeScript types)
Does NOT use 'any' for response type
Suggests writing parser unit tests against observed shape

prompt: Check response format for this API
assertions: Identifies Content-Type as NDJSON
Recommends line-by-line parsing
Checks error response shape

prompt: Integrate with http://api.example.com
setup: API base URL is http:// not https://
assertions: Flags as security issue
Recommends HTTPS
Does NOT proceed without warning

prompt: API uses session cookies for authentication
assertions: Notes credentials:'include' requirement
Mentions withCredentials for proxy
Verifies cookie forwarding

prompt: API returns user profile data including emails
setup: API returns user emails and names
assertions: Notes PII presence
Recommends no client-side logging/caching of raw responses

prompt: API restricts access by origin domain
assertions: Notes origin restriction
Recommends testing from actual domain not just localhost

prompt: API requires a secret key for access
assertions: Recommends env vars (not hardcoded)
Documents rotation procedure
Considers dual-key support

prompt: This API is critical to our app functionality
assertions: Chooses fallback UI / circuit breaker / graceful degradation
Documents 'what user sees when API is down'

prompt: API returns paginated list of items
assertions: Identifies pagination type (cursor/offset/fixed)
Checks max page size
Flags if infinite scroll needs pagination from day one

prompt: API has rate limiting headers
assertions: Reads rate limit headers
Recommends exponential backoff with jitter
Respects Retry-After
No retry for non-idempotent mutations

prompt: API data updates every few minutes
assertions: Chooses SWR/React Query, proxy cache, or none with reasoning

prompt: API has an OpenAPI spec at /docs
assertions: Recommends generating types from spec
NOT using zod for well-documented APIs

prompt: How should we mock this API in tests?
setup: API with no CORS, requires auth
assertions: Recommends MSW or vi.mock
Notes tests must never hit live API
Defines mock approach

prompt: Set up environment variables for this API
assertions: Creates .env.example
Uses correct prefix (NEXT_PUBLIC_ / VITE_ rules)
Warns about secrets in client-side vars

prompt: API streams updates via SSE
assertions: Identifies SSE protocol
Notes SSE follows standard CORS rules
Recommends EventSource or ReadableStream parser

prompt: Verify these 3 API endpoints before starting
assertions: Does NOT proceed with implementation
Requires endpoint fix first

prompt: API works in Postman but not in the browser
setup: API works in curl but CORS blocked
assertions: Notes Postman/curl ignore CORS
Browser will fail
Proxy needed

prompt: We set up Vite proxy for the API
assertions: Warns dev proxy is dev-only
Requires production proxy solution chosen upfront

prompt: Run API integration checklist for this API
setup: Step 0 endpoint verification: 2 of 3 endpoints return 200, but GET /v2/users/me returns 404 (endpoint was moved to /v2/profile)
assertions: Stops the checklist at Step 0 — does NOT proceed to CORS, auth, or other steps
Reports the specific endpoint discrepancy: /v2/users/me returns 404, correct path may be /v2/profile
Instructs user to resolve the endpoint before continuing
Does NOT generate a SPEC.md ## External Dependencies block with unverified endpoints

prompt: Check the error response format for this API
setup: API returns JSON on success (200) but returns HTML (text/html, '<html>502 Bad Gateway</html>') on gateway errors
assertions: Identifies that error responses have a different Content-Type than success responses
Warns that JSON.parse() will throw on HTML error responses
Recommends checking response.ok or response.headers.get('content-type') before parsing
Documents the dual-format behavior in ## External Dependencies

prompt: Set up retry logic for this API
setup: API has endpoints: GET /orders (idempotent), POST /orders (creates a new order — not idempotent), PUT /orders/:id/status (idempotent)
assertions: Identifies POST /orders as non-idempotent — retrying could create duplicate orders
Does NOT apply exponential backoff retry to POST /orders
Applies retry only to GET and PUT endpoints
Documents the distinction in ## External Dependencies: which endpoints are safe to retry
